Narrative_Meaning: Queens signify an older female, matriarchy, scandals, social events, curiosity, love, wisdom, sinister manipulations and betrayal.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Cloak card is symbolic of the warmth and protection of a good coat, privileges of rank, and the hidden aspects of life. This can be those parts that are private, or actual secrets, perhaps even hidden passages, or secret tunnels. It is the card of secrets, thieves and cowards, as well as nobility, cunning, and superheroes.

Significator.Plot: The Cloak Plot is usually a Spy or Superhero Story, like the Mask. Unlike the Mask, The Cloak is about secrets, and privileges, not dual identities, so while the Characters' secrets may be threatened, they do not have to live as another Side or person. Cloak Plots will usually kill Embodiments on all sides, but often new Embodiments will be recruited during the Plot
Significator.Side: A Cloaked side may be better protected or privileged than normal, or they may be secretive, hidden from view, indeed they can be so cloaked their Embodiments may not obviously appear, like the DeeperShadows Side. When the Cloak marks the DeeperShadows the DeeperShadows may actually Embody and act, although they will suicide rather than reveal anything about themselves if captured (which would be very unlikely anyway).
Significator.Character:
The Cloak Significator indicates a Character who keeps their real self hidden.

They Gain Yin when they hide or avoid conversation.

They Gain Yang when they lie or tell a story.

They Gain Chi when they ignore their Persona.

They also gain +1 Success Level to any hide actions, or remaining hidden.
Significator.Scene: The Cloak Scene is any Scene where what actually happens is in some way hidden, or misleading. A Yarn-Teller may play the Cloak to hide themselves or others, granting them an ambush, escape or a rest, as required. The Cloak allows them to make secret actions with little chance of detection.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets one of the Character's Hitches, usually a Flaw. For example: In order to Hook a Duty (Honour-bound) Hitch simply threaten the Character's honour, or have them swear to do something.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Hook tells the Character directly of some looming, but secretive danger, rumours of a new mob boss, prototype fighter with incredible stats, or any mysterious stranger or player.
Yarn.Gain: Secret Alliance (the creation of a secretive Pact Descendant)
Yarn.Fray_Description: These Ordeals may involve hiding, or concealing something. The PCs may have to hide themselves from detection, or they may have to conceal something else, perhaps not letting the guards take the kid who just burst into the room and hid under the sofa. As such Hiding can be as much about bluffing as it is physical concealment, but is an Opposed Ordeal of almost any Stakes.
Yarn.Snag_Description: Whatever happened during the Fray, there is no one to bear witness to the result. This means the truth of what happened will be clouded by speculation and suspicions, mostly directed at the PCs. Alternatively, the Fray is complicated by the PCs having to insure they are not spotted, and that their involvement remains secret.

NPC.Description: Rogues are scoundrels, who rarely attack in a fair fight. Ambushes and sniping tactics are more their Style, perhaps with a little smuggling, pickpocketing or burglary along the way.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs may gain an actual cloak of invisibility, or more likely they gain a Revelation that they could not have got any other way, perhaps overhearing or intercepting a communication from the Opposed Embodiment.
Yarn.History_Description: The Cloak indicates that the history of this situation is shrouded, this may mean that the truth cannot be known, or that the truth is known, but not widely shared. Unlike the Mask there is no cover story or fiction, just lingering mysteries and hidden facts.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Cloak indicates that the current situation involves hiding or being hidden. This may involve an enemy force hidden with-in the lord's territory, or having to hide yourselves or some contraband. Occasionally the Cloak will indicate a hidden force, advantage, or strength that is being overlooked.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Cloak indicates that the solution to the current situation is most often to hide, and remain unseen, while observing yourself. This may reveal information that will be useful, allow you to ambush your enemies, or simply flee the area undetected.

Revelations.About_Description: The Information is about some secret. This might be a secret group, door, passageway or perhaps information like a password.
Revelations.Info_Description: Obscure information is information that is difficult to find, sometimes this is because someone would prefer the information was not available at all, other times it is because the information is genuinely difficult to find out. Examples might include, finding out the real name of the Dark Lord, or the exact frequencies that will negate that battleship's Electronic Counter Measures.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Clothing can convey a lot of information about a person or culture, from the level of trade and wealth they have to specific details of kinship, or favourite TV show.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about the comforts that the Embodiment enjoys, these are usually specific, rather than generic comforts, perhaps that character is known to sleep only in an oxygen tent, on duckling eiderdown, or with a teddy bear, a Monster may take comfort in eating only those people who are overweight, or those who are exceptionally healthy, or bathes daily in a specific spring, pool or grotto, or perhaps a Descendant must be cleaned only with neat grain alcohol, must kept in a secure but silk-lined, rose-oak case, or must be polished with only wax from a particular region's bees.
Revelations.Detail_Description: A hidden detail is placed deliberately and always carries a communication of some sort, whether it is a hidden maker's mark, the references to other movies in a scene's set dressing, an invisible ink, an easily overlooked microdot, or an engineered quantum signature. These are the details that may be obvious if you are looking for them, but are unnoticeable if you are unaware.

Obstacle.Description: The way ahead is hidden or secret. Secret doors, hidden trapdoors, wall safes and the like, must be searched or spotted to find.

Tarot.Normal: Add a rolled Score to the Score the Target generates again (x2/x3) this may allow more cards to be Drawn, etc. while atop the pile.
Tarot.Crossed: Halves all Scores the Target generates whilst atop their pile.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 1 per Stage and 2 per Court Stage
Ordeal.Stage_Description: Evade or dodge something, come up with a story, a Blending in opportunity, or Medical emergency.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: If you can fly then you might stand a chance, if you aren't gifted with wings you are going to have to climb, swing, balance and jump.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing forces believe they are on the brink of success, and this causes them to potentially make a mistake, revealing themselves from cover, dropping their defences, or pushing into attack too hard (+1 Flowing Failure Level).
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The fight is normally a Low or Soul Stakes argument between two Characters (at least Chorus Extra). The opponent may be a Monster of some sort.
  Facet    Monster  
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: These paths open and close naturally through the Lea from time to time, they are easily walked by all Bulmäs, but Vermis and All-Walkers may walk them in Swarm or True Dragon configurations.

Stress.Description: Stress can make monsters of us all, but worse still Stress can make Monsters, not just mountains, out of molehills.
Stress.Rules: The Character must pay Chi equal to the Character’s current Stress as Yarn. This Chi is used to purchase a new Character that can be a Force-of-Nature Extra with Monster Facets (Enigma, Heresy, Jeer, Liberty, Phoenix, and Nature). These "Monsters" may actually be physical issues such as natural disasters, or they may be aspects of the Characters own Psyche, given hallucinatory or supernatural form. Then again they can be the Character themselves, unaware in their Stress induced black-outs what they are doing. Other times the Character will project their problems on an actual Monster, tying their Arcs together. The only catharsis available to a Character here is to defeat, or at least survive the Monster, which will relieve Stress equal to the Monster Facet Boon.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can sometimes hide other problems. For example, during an accident a Character may snap a limb obviously, they are only concerned about the blinding pain coming from the broken limb. Only once the limb is set does the Character (or the medics treating them) become aware of the muscle damage in their back, or the concussion.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma hides a Wound so that it cannot be seen or treated (effectively filling the same Wound Slot with another Wound). The Cloaking Wound is created by relieving Stress, with the Stress relieved equalling the Pips of the Wound, a Limit Card is usually Drawn and Played, that adds Suit and Wound Limit for the Pips. Once this Cloaking Wound has been treated and healed away, the Cloaked Wound will reappear in that Wound Slot. Both the Cloaking Wound and Cloaked Wound occupy the same Wound Slot at once, but the Cloaked Wounds effects and damage are hidden until healed. While a Character has a Cloaking Wound, they may relieve Stress at any time by adding the Pips to their Cloaking Wound, making it potentially Fester, Re-Open or even get worsse as they must Draw a new Limit Card that will replace the old Limit if Higher.

Age.Description: An Age of Secrets is occurring. During this age a society becomes secretive and self-protecting. A society in an age of secrets may make the society into a secret society, with membership never revealed beyond the society, but this is not guarranteed. Secrecy may protect the society from some external threat, or it may be intended to keep some secret, such as a source, technique or technology to maintain a monopoly. Historically the greatest example is probably the silk trade, which caused the nation of China to maintain an age of Secrets, protecting knowledge of the silk worm for around a millenium.
Age.Rules: Certain information within or about the society is protected by a veil of secrecy, how far the society will go to keep those secrets will vary with the secret and society, but most will consider killing more acceptable than dying to preserve a secret.
